revision_summary: Three targeted fixes required: (1) SCHEMA MISUSE — cherry_pick_moments[3]: reclassify from 'empathy_miss / empathy_on_vulnerability_disclosure' to a listening/misread failure. The metal roof warranty is a practical constraint, not a vulnerability disclosure per the schema definition. Update schema_reference to coaching_philosophy.setter_scope_discipline or analysis_directives.knowledge_gap_handling. Remove 'tone-deaf' characterization and replace with behavioral description. (2) WEAK REASONING — trajectory_verdict.outcome_dispute: qualify the homeowner status assertion. The transcript implies ownership but Bucky never ran the verify sequence — flag as 'inferred, not confirmed' rather than stating it as established fact. (3) MISSED MOMENTS — add two cherry_pick entries: (a) ts 02:27, Bucky's in-house roofing warranty pivot — classify as scope_creep/misrouted_cross_sell, cite cross_sell_signals schema section, note that the correct pattern is routing to the roofing team not pitching roofing capabilities directly; (b) ts 00:55, customer's location question as a legitimacy probe — classify as missed trust_building_micro_trade, note Bucky had the customer's utility (Northern Neck Electric, a Virginia cooperative) as a regional anchor he didn't use. Also correct the flipped_hypothesis reasoning at 02:23: the rejection of the strategic read is correct but the stated reason ('customer interrupted') is factually wrong — Bucky trailed off and the customer responded with 'Mhmm' (an acknowledgment), not an interruption; the self-close at 02:55 came after a full exchange, not immediately after the trailing sentence.
